message:
Replace remark-partial-headings with remark-inline-partials

Inline partial content directly into the document tree so that
headings from partials participate in page TOC and automatic
anchor links. This approach is simpler and doesn't require a
separate generated map.

- Rename plugin from remark-partial-headings to remark-inline-partials
- Add verify-inline-partials script to validate partial imports
- Update configuration and remove generated directory from .gitignore
- Update CLAUDE.md with documentation about partial behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -55,6 +55,10 @@ import ParsingOptions from '@partials/operators/ParsingOptions.mdx';
 <ParsingOptions />
 ```
 
+Partials are inlined during Markdown processing, so their headings participate
+in the page TOC and automatic anchor links. Only default imports that resolve
+inside `/src/partials/` are inlined; other component imports behave normally.
+
 ## Workflow
 
 ### Development Server

@@ -32,9 +32,6 @@ tenzir-docs.md
 # Excalidraw generated SVGs (foo.excalidraw -> foo.excalidraw.svg)
 *.excalidraw.svg
 
-# Partial headings map (generated by remark-partial-headings)
-src/generated/
-
 # Logs
 dev.log
